channel.title: Comments for page "CASE COLOURLESS GREEN"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "CASE COLOURLESS GREEN"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another masterpiece with antimemetics stuff. I wish you best luck, and hope you make more of these tales as they make me go WOAH and i believe others feel same too

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. "Finish what you start" is the number one piece of advice I give to people. Even serious professional writers fail hard at this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I decided to reread the entire series after finishing the entire thing, because damn, what a ride. for me, this series is up there with the infected town series from r/nosleep, another story that took years to complete and had a great ensemble cast, and that's far and away a favorite even among favorites. (i feel the same way about this series.)

"who's Adam?" (quietly sobs in retrospect)

thanks again for sticking it out, sticking the landing, and giving us an ending. it's a bummer how many stories with great potential are never given endings. (not that I'm entitled to them as someone reading this content for free, lol, I'm just saying.)

item.title: Amazingly done all around
item.content:encoded:
Multiple tales tying multiple SCPs together. Great "webbing" between articles and successful crosslinking between all of the articles mentioned.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want you to know, I love you, and I love this. I'm not usually into tales, they weren't my thing, but you brought me in and now I can't stop. I'm moving onto the next story, and so help me god if I encounter a stopping point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn. I love this all so much. You're gonna have to write a road map to what to read and in what order at the end of this, though!

And a question: Will 55 be involved at all? In the other tales it's an antimeme so powerful it eats ghost-foundation operative-meme hybrids so actually finding out what it is and how it relates to this big horror thing would be so satisfying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Leave it as is. Honestly, that's probably where I stole the idea from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad somebody finally spotted that >:) That's the main reason why I chose that number even though I know we'll hit series 3000 sometime soon, rather than something higher. When the time comes I'll write it — the "real" version — up properly as an SCP entry, although it'll probably end up retreading lots of ground from this Tale.

In my head — and I know some of the people who've also been writing antimemetics stuff have different takes on this, so I probably won't make it "official" in a Tale, but in my head — the "missing integer" described in SCP-033 is also the number 5. It's just a different number 5, an abhorrent, brain-breaking discontinuity in elementary arithmetic which doesn't strictly exist, but is kind of a manifestation of SCP-3125 which sort of lodges itself in the thoughts of mathematicians. It may or may not have a different sigil from our usual 5.

I was tempted to have Marion discover a few piece of paper in Cognitohazard Containment Unit 3125, with torn-off edges and 5 scrawled on it. Possibly even two of them, as a pointer to link all of this up with SCP-055. I decided not to in the end because it was making the story too "busy", if you see what I mean. It wasn't directly supporting the main arc of the narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to point out that 55 = 3125…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a third party, this seems more like a "Great minds think alike"/"Existential horror nerds read alike" kind of situation.

Also, qntm doesn't say CASE COLORLESS GREEN anywhere in-universe, it's just a (good) evocative title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you think that I should change Incident-COLORLESS IDEA (for the reference to the Grey incident in SCP-2111) to something else? I had no idea that you were about to source a codename from the same well. Is this embarrassing or irrelevant? I can't decide. Send help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The missing part of the phrase, "…ideas sleep furiously", is also highly relevant because SCP-3125 is an extremely dangerous, dormant meme complex — or, if you will, a furiously sleeping idea, now woken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Charlie Stross, you friggin' philistines XD
((Seriously, his stuff is amazing.))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you have.

Ah, it's the Laundry Files. I stored that by title, not author. That's where the oddity surfaced. Occasionally, some things fall through my understanding, even after going through it a few times, like angular momentum and the name Amy.

ETA: Frickin' heck, he's the githyanki guy. Okay, yes, he's definitely someone in my Amy blind spot. (A few years back, while discussing names, I observed that I knew of the name Amy, but not of anyone named Amy. This, despite knowing about celebrities like Amy Acker and Amy Winehouse - who I just so happened to remember by their last names only. It was a very real blind spot.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually never heard of Charlie Stoss.

That's weird, I mentioned his work in relation to your On Mount Golgotha project…

Anyway CASE [something] GREEN (allcaps) is pretty obviously a reference to the Laundry Files.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still hear the echo of Stross in there, combined with the fact that one of the GOIs (the GOC, I think) refers to bixbys as "Type Greens" - and it sounds like nothing short of a bixby can help at this point… :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was about to say that it is probably both.

What's interesting is that I also referenced that Chomsky sentence in SCP-2111 and I have been debating changing it to something else after this story was posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And/or Noam Chomsky.

The sentence "Colorless green ideas sleep furiously" was forwarded by him to show how a phrase could be grammatically correct, but still carry no meaningful information.

ETA: Thus, it could be simultaneously a future apocalyptic event, and a case of a situation impossible to understand.
(Yup, modded to combine interpretations. I actually never heard of Charlie Stoss.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great but it gave me a small taste of that "boring invincible villain" fatigue, even though technically the events described are realistic in this setting.

Is the title a reference to Charlie Stoss? CASE NIGHTMARE GREEN is the near future apocalyptic event in The Laundry series.

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded:
I also like how SCP-055 was contained when it was published. Does this mean all the anti-memetic stories take place in real time (at the time of their publication)?

Not exactly. The next Tale picks up mere minutes after "CASE COLOURLESS GREEN" but obviously I haven't published it yet…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The antimememtic tales are consistently some of the most original writing in the Foundation Universe. Kudos, and I can't wait to see where this goes.

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded:
I love this tale, and I love the anti-memetics concept and what you've done with it; it adds an extra layer of depth to the Foundation universe without feeling forced, cheesy, or cliche, especially compared to certain other things on the site, like certain GOIs.

Horror comes from fear of the unknown, so writing tales about the unknowable is the next logical step. I think that's why this all works so well.

Your focus on the Foundation as a scientific organization does wonders to maintain one's suspension of disbelief when reading stories like this.

I also like how SCP-055 was contained when it was published. Does this mean all the anti-memetic stories take place in real time (at the time of their publication)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have the same concern; I think there's actually only a finite amount of space in this "antimemetics universe" and I've been trying really hard to avoid repeating myself/outstaying my welcome. I'm starting to become sick of the term "antimemetics" itself, semantic satiation is starting to set in for me, and I know I'm not the only one who feels that way. That's why I'm glad I designed this as a fixed-length story, which is now almost over.

Oh, and thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every time you write a tale, qntm, I ask myself "Jesus, do we really need another anti-memetic whatsit?" And every time I read one of your tales, I answer "Yes, yes we do."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 loved this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If 3125 isn't the source of all antimemetic anomalies, which besides 055 and Marion's memory-eater are outside its purview, if even these are?

No comment re SCP-055.

I wasn't able to fit this into the story, but I had the idea that Marion's memory-eater (SCP-4987) might have been another deliberate attack. I feel as if SCP-3125 has a variety of defence mechanisms; SCP-4987 could be one of these, used relatively rarely against only the most stubborn resistance. There could have been other instances deployed against other antimemetics groups. (Incidentally, SCP-4987 is even worse than it sounds at face value because it has a strong preference for consuming happy memories over neutral ones, and it will never consume unhappy memories, which means the longer Marion is bound to it the more miserable her life gets. I didn't have the time to fully explore this angle though.)

For an example of something not caused by SCP-3125: SCP-2256 :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.
All right, qntm, we'll wait! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty good, but 3125 doesn't really feel like a meme so much as a mind-control monster. It's too overt. I like the concept, and I look forward to seeing where this goes, but it still doesn't feel like a meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1425 is the opposite of antimemetic.

There is the thing about the whole bit of widespread insanity that apparently everybody just forgot happened.

If 3125 isn't the source of all antimemetic anomalies, which besides 055 and Marion's memory-eater are outside its purview, if even these are?

SCP-2256 appears to have a been a naturally evolved organism.

The goblins of SCP-2523 aren't beholden to any eldritch power other than themselves, and probably have other worlds to exploit if this one is eaten.

MTF Omega-Zero is actively engaged in the war against SCP-3125.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I interpreted it as 3125 and the object the Fifthists worship being one and the same. The Fifthist Church is definitely a byproduct of a larger invasion, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the impression that the tale conjectures that the Fifthist Church is a byproduct of 3125, and not actually made or sent by 3125 itself. That, or the eldritch horror/mind-shaping anomaly/glitch in the Universe that results in the thing that Fifthists "worship" (for lack of a better word) is a byproduct of 3125, not made by it itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good story, but I have a few questions and conjectures -

Wheeler's recorded narrative gave me the impression that 3125's murderous qualities are an instantaneous death impulse, like the cognitohazard fractals featured in 01 proposals and other entries. But then it turns out all the antagonists in the Antimemetics series are just antimemetic agents of this larger badness sent (ineffectually with Wheeler, so far) to kill antimemeticists. A bit confusing.

1425 is the opposite of antimemetic. In fact it's rather typical of the old fashioned memetic concept-virus that was in vogue several years ago. From your telling, the implication seems to be that 3125 is either the force or entity normally worshipped by the Fifth Church, and it guides or manipulates them into spreading its influence among humanity, or, less plausibly, 1425 was just a one-off attempt by 3125 to co-opt the Fifth Church's logistics and infrastructure to spread its power. Does it make much sense to anyone else for the god of secret demons to also create an anomalously popular star cult led by Hollywood?

If 3125 isn't the source of all antimemetic anomalies, which besides 055 and Marion's memory-eater are outside its purview, if even these are?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was good to a degree that I cannot express with only a single upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In case anybody's following along at home, this Tale took me two solid months to write, and the version you're reading here is draft number thirty.

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3125 is the designation for the root cause of numerous phenomena in the Foundation universe, of which:

  • some are anomalous
  • some have antimemetic properties
  • some are known to the Foundation
  • some are known the Antimemetics division
  • some have appeared in these Tales.

(All of the sets are different and overlap to different degrees.) SCP-3125 itself hasn't been directly mentioned before, but many of its manifestations have. However, SCP-3125 is definitely not the source of all antimemetic phenomena, any more than the Earth is the source of all gravity. In my view this kind of catchall explanation shrinks the universe and makes it less interesting. It also constrains the storytelling possibilities as we move forward. There need to be other ways to get antimemes, otherwise that's just dull and it ties everybody's hands.

(Did you notice that SCP-3125 is a monster which eats people who try to find a common origin for too many other SCPs? This is deliberate!)

In this Tale, Wheeler claims (and she is probably correct, although not infallible) that the following are manifestations/symptoms/injuries to our reality ultimately traceable back to SCP-3125:

  • SCP-1425
  • SCP-033
  • SCP-2828
  • the massive invisible spider entity which ate Marness in "Unforgettable, That's What You Are"

I will also go on record saying that

  • Clay in "We Need To Talk About Fifty-Five"
  • Grey in "Introductory Antimemetics"

originate from SCP-3125; in fact, they were antimemetic weapons deployed against the Antimemetics division as part of this ongoing war (to which the division was oblivious at the time).

I thought about this a little and I'm going to go ahead and say

  • The Contagious Murder Of Bartholomew Hughes

is another manifestation of SCP-3125. Note that this murder took place "seven years" ago, i.e. in 2008. This was the year that SCP-055 was originally contained. It's also the year that global antimemetic science apparently peaked, as Wheeler implies in this Tale. That seems to be the year that (this iteration of) the war began.

item.title: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded: Is SCP-3125 the "contagious murder" from We Need to Talk About Fifty-Five or is it some unrelated SCP that hasn't been mentioned before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am amazed at the speed in wich you put up quality writings. Amazing work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I said 'Oh fuck no, they can't stop here!!!!!!!!!!!!'

Forget about real life, qntm, we want, nay, we NEED to know what happens next!!!!!!

<zooms off to read 1425>
<zooms back> Already read it before! <zooms off>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very, very good. I actually said, "Awww!" out loud when there wasn't more to read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic as always. The story is getting more intense as it goes, and I enjoy the "mass extinction of antimemeticists" thread more than the "antimeme bomb" thread from the last one.

Edit: I should also mention that it's very interesting that the adversary's responses are getting more physical, in real body-horror ways. Pen-knife imagery… yegh.

Edit edit: Also, Laundry Files reference in the title, I assume? If so, bonus points.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This kind of Cognitohazard Containment Unit is also referred to as a Vegas Room, because what happens there stays there.

Wheeler was privately hoping that it contained a secret spa for Antimemetics division chiefs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was unsure at first, but I think this is a really clever extension of the antimeme idea. It's such an impossible problem, and I'm very interested to see where this goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was fantastic in every way. I especially loved the bits about the architect - it added a huge amount of depth and believability to a very conceptually dense piece. Can't wait to see the next part.

item.title: Re: I really like this concept. Minor issue.
item.content:encoded:
That's what happens to speech when this thing infects you in order to destroy those who know.

Also, a hlai is a large flightless bird, native to the world ch'Rihan (Romulus), and raised by Rihannsu (Romulan) farmers as a meat animal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Continuing to love the antimemetics stuff. Keep up the good work.

item.title: Re: I really like this concept. Minor issue.
item.content:encoded: If the second thing is intentional, then whatever. I may just be inattentive.

item.title: Re: I really like this concept. Minor issue.
item.content:encoded:
The first thing you mentioned was broken markup, now fixed, thank you.

The second thing is deliberate. It refers to SCP-1425. If it doesn't make sense out of context then I need to make it clearer that something weird's going on with how people are speaking.

item.title: I really like this concept. Minor issue.
item.content:encoded:
I believe there are typos and a broken link here:

I'm on so many mnestic drugs that my endocrine system is shutting down. Taking amnestics at the same time is the chemical equivalent to trepanation. I don't remember the last time I slept without having a nightmare about Adam, and I'm starting to forget whether [[[we-need-to-talk-about-fifty-five|SCP-4987]]

I don't know what this means:

and you trwoll have to figure a way out of this

"There's something inl fleth on your neck

I think hlai you've been

If these are purposeful, then I didn't catch it.
